exemplar.title: How does a tension of a violin string affect its fundamental frequency?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does a tension of a violin string affect its fundamental frequency?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are clear and precise, with consistent units and significant figures
Sample calculations (means and standard deviations) are well demonstrated
High RÂ² value (0.999) is correctly reported, indicating strong statistical analysis
weaknesses: Error bars are omitted without quantitative justification
Uncertainty propagation in regression slope and intercept is not shown
Data are fitted linearly rather than testing the theoretical square-root relationship
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data Analysis communication is both clear and precise (band 5), but consideration of uncertainties and processing quality each exhibit significant omissions (band 3). A balanced holistic mark of 4 acknowledges excellent presentation alongside important analytical gaps.

strengths: Comprehensive description of apparatus and equipment supports reproducibility
Clear and concise statement of the research question with specific violin context
Engaging personal narrative that strengthens contextual framing
weaknesses: Balance calibration method and microphone placement not specified
Hypothesis statement is incomplete, lacking explicit mathematical form
Control of string length under varying tension is not fully explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research Design strands show high-quality contextualization (band 5) and solid procedural detail (band 4), but methodological considerations are only described rather than fully explained (band 3). A holistic score of 4 reflects this mix of strong context and reproducibility offset by gaps in calibration, hypothesis articulation, and control details.

strengths: Conclusion accurately summarizes the observed linear trend in the data
weaknesses: Conclusion contradicts the theoretical square-root dependence and lacks reconciliation
No quantitative justification or uncertainty discussion included
Comparison to accepted scientific context is superficial and undeveloped
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is consistent with the studentâs analysis (band 3) but fails to align with theoretical expectations and offers only a cursory comparison to scientific context (band 1). A score of 2 reflects mid-band performance weakened by these critical inconsistencies.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified, demonstrating reflective critique
Realistic and relevant improvements (e.g., automated plucker, professional microphone) are proposed
Further research suggestions are thoughtful and extend the investigationâs scope
weaknesses: Relative impact of listed errors is not quantified or prioritized
Explanations of how improvements mitigate errors are brief rather than thorough
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation lists specific limitations and realistic improvements (band 3 for both strands) but lacks quantitative analysis of error impacts and deeper explanation of enhancements. A holistic score of 3 accurately represents this mid-band performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student situates the question of how tension affects the fundamental frequency of a violin âAâ string within the very specific context of violin tuning, referencing pizzicato technique, standard violin scale length and everyday tuning practice.  This goes beyond a broad mention and clearly identifies the physical system, the circumstances of use and the theoretical model to be tested, matching the descriptor for the top band.  Minor narrative digressions do not detract from the appropriateness of the context, so a mark in the upper band is warranted, albeit not the maximum because some contextual statements (e.g., ârelationship not well-knownâ) are factually weak.

Uncertainties are calculated for mass per unit length, tension (Â±0.5 N) and frequency (combined SD and instrument error).  However, tension error is later dismissed as âinsignificantâ, error bars are omitted from the graph, and propagation of uncertainty in the gradient is not shown.  The consideration is therefore present but with significant omissions, matching the 3-4 band at a mid-level score.

Variables are identified and a table of controls is supplied, but the explanation of why each control matters is brief and sometimes generic.  Eleven tension points with five repeats provide sufficient data, yet the student does not justify the selected range against safe operating limits or expected functional form.  Methodological considerations are therefore âdescribedâ rather than fully âexplainedâ, placing the work in the middle of the 3-4 band.

The evaluation table lists specific weaknesses (variable pluck force, possible deformation, movement of stabilisers, reading fluctuations).  Each is briefly described, but the relative impact on results is only asserted as ânot significantâ without quantitative support.  Hence the student âdescribes specific methodological weaknessesâ, consistent with the 3-4 band.

The procedure (sonometer, spring balance, phone oscilloscope, 5 N steps from 100â150 N, five trials) is described in enough detail that a competent peer could reproduce the investigation, but some ambiguities remain: distance of microphone, exact placement of bridges, calibration method for the spring balance and phone app are not given.  These omissions are âfewâ rather than âmanyâ, so the work fits comfortably in the 3-4 band, at the top of that band.

Means, standard deviations and linear regression are carried out correctly; nevertheless, the theoretical model predicts f â âT, yet the student forces a linear fit without transforming variables or testing the expected relationship.  This conceptual inaccuracy constitutes a âsignificant omissionâ in processing quality, so band 3-4 with a score of 3 is appropriate.

For every listed weakness the student proposes a realistic and relevant improvement (use electric violin, automated plucker, multiple strings, professional microphone, alternative force gauge).  Explanations of how the improvements would mitigate the errors are brief rather than thorough, so the descriptor âdescribedâ (not âexplainedâ) applies, meriting a mid-band score.

Rawâ and processed-data tables include titles, units, uncertainties and appropriate significant figures; graphs have labelled axes, linear fit and RÂ².  The presentation is both clear (easy to read) and precise (units/decimal places consistent).  Minor formatting glitches (mis-typed â520.â) prevent perfection, but the descriptors for band 5-6 are largely satisfied.

Apart from an initial citation of the textbook formula, no systematic comparison is made between experimental findings and accepted theory; discrepancies are neither recognised nor addressed.  The comparison therefore remains superficial, fitting the 1-2 band at the lower end.

The student concludes that frequency is linearly related to tension and states that this supports the hypothesis.  The conclusion is consistent with the data analysis performed, but it contradicts the earlier theoretical background (square-root dependence) and is not quantitatively justified through uncertainty discussion.  This aligns with the descriptor âdescribed but not fully consistentâ, earning mid-band marks.

annotations.comment: The further research suggestions are realistic and relevant, proposing extensions to other strings and materials to generalize the relationship.
The evaluation table lists specific sources of error and improvements but lacks discussion of their relative impact. Explain which errors most strongly influence the gradient and result accuracy.
Realistic and relevant improvements are proposed (e.g., automated plucker, professional microphone), demonstrating thoughtful consideration of methodological enhancements.
Clear and concise statement of the research question at the start of the document. This focus helps the reader immediately grasp the aim of the investigation.

Consider adding a brief contextual sentence immediately after the research question here to frame it within violin tuning practice, such as standard tuning frequencies and why this relationship matters to performers.
The personal narrative in the introduction effectively engages the reader and establishes student motivation. This strengthens the contextual framing of the investigation.
The statement ârelationship is not well-knownâ is factually weak. Cite a source or clarify what aspect is poorly understood to substantiate this claim and avoid overgeneralization.
The derivation and presentation of the fundamental frequency formula $$f = \frac{1}{2L}\sqrt{\frac{T}{\mu}}$$ is clear and accurate, demonstrating solid theoretical grounding.
The hypothesis statement is cut off mid-sentence and lacks completion. Ensure the hypothesis is fully articulated, specifying the expected mathematical relationship based on theory.
While mass per unit length is calculated, the balance calibration method and systematic check against known masses are not described. Include detail on how the balance was zeroed and verified.
The fractional uncertainty calculation is presented correctly, but dismissing <1% as âstatistically insignificantâ is premature without comparing propagated uncertainties in final results.
The variables section precisely identifies independent, dependent, and controlled variables, aiding reproducibility and clarity of the experimental design.
In the control table, the method to maintain string length under variable tension is unclear. Explain precisely how stabilizers prevent length change when tension is incrementally increased.
The explanation of apparatus and list of equipment is comprehensive and clearly laid out, which supports reproducibility of the experiment.
Distance and orientation of the phoneâs microphone relative to the string are not specified. Include a measurement of separation distance to ensure consistent acoustic detection.
The raw data table includes five repeats, uncertainty values, and proper units, satisfying clear and precise data recording.
Error bars were omitted from the graph as âinsignificantâ but quantitative justification is absent. Display error bars or show that propagated uncertainties are truly negligible.
The sample calculation clearly demonstrates mean and standard deviation formulas, which aids understanding of data processing steps.
Propagation of uncertainty in the gradient is not shown. Include calculations for uncertainties in slope and intercept to fully assess data processing quality.
The processed data table is well formatted with consistent significant figures and units, supporting precise communication of results.
The tension uncertainty is treated as negligible without propagating its effect into frequency uncertainty. Integrate ÎT into the uncertainty budget to validate this assumption.
Reporting an RÂ² of 0.999 highlights strong correlation, demonstrating precise use of statistical metrics.
Fitting frequency directly against tension contradicts the theoretical model $$f\propto\sqrt{T}$$. Transform data (e.g., plot f vs âT) to test the square-root relationship.
Plotting maximum and minimum best-fit lines explores uncertainty ranges, showcasing thoughtful consideration of data variability.
Using only the first three points to illustrate slope extremes reduces statistical significance. Recalculate extremes including all data points or justify this subset choice.
The conclusion correctly summarizes the observed linear trend but contradicts the theoretical square-root dependence. Reconcile findings with the theoretical model or discuss reasons for deviation.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
